goal: gad tui must NEVER be blocked from launching by a stale router lockfile. Repro 2026-04-22: gad tui exits with '[router] lockfile exists at .gad/router-logs/.lock — pid=26520 is still running' when PID 26520 is in fact NOT alive (process exited 5+ hours earlier; lockfile orphaned because previous tui crashed/killed without cleanup). Fix: in the router init code that reads .gad/router-logs/.lock, after parsing pid, probe with process.kill(pid, 0) (POSIX) or tasklist /FI 'PID eq <pid>' (Windows) to verify liveness. If pid not running, log '[router] stale lockfile (pid=X dead, age=Ns) — clearing' and unlink + proceed. Operator-facing rule: gad tui is the operator's primary interactive surface; treat any block on launch as a P0 bug. Same lockfile-staleness pattern likely affects gad team dispatcher pid file (.planning/team/dispatcher.pid) — sweep all pid-file consumers in vendor for the same defensive probe.
